constraints: Preserve reconstructive React/CSS output and avoid image-based shortcuts.
Keep Storybook and example parity through shared source paths rather than forks.
Improve dark mode and accessibility-sensitive states without flattening the visual system.
Prefer shared reusable code/editor/diff and mockup controls over page-local wrappers.
gaps: Several visually important surfaces were recently extracted into primitives, but the broad convergence goal still required the example-app controls and docs code figure to move into shared kit APIs rather than staying app/page-local.

Dark-mode and accessibility quality are generally improved, but most hierarchy still depends on shared CSS vocabulary rather than deeply prop-driven variants, so each newly extracted surface must preserve those states carefully.
Storybook coverage needed to expose shared controls and newly extracted motifs directly, not only through composed page previews.
reusableSurfaces: Seraph primitives including specimen and ledger variants
Codex docs/dashboard primitives including docs code figures
specAnchors: shared code surface source in src/components/Code

exampleSurfaces: examples/basic-usage uses shared MockupPreviewControls and MockupPreviews
docs and Seraph previews now consume shared extracted primitives through the same runtime path
fixesApplied: Promoted multiple previously page-local surfaces into reusable kit APIs: Seraph specimen plate, staggered Seraph ledger/proof band, and Codex docs code figure.
Extended code and diff surfaces with richer file-level metadata framing and improved Storybook control coverage for broader docs/chat/mock reuse.

Extracted the mockup gallery control panel into a shared MockupPreviewControls component, moved its styling into shared package CSS, and rewired the example app to consume the shared control surface instead of duplicating local UI.
residualRisks: Further gains from here would be incremental breadth work rather than closing a current reuse blocker.
Dark-mode hierarchy remains largely CSS-vocabulary driven, but the extracted surfaces preserve existing theme-aware behavior and did not introduce new obvious contrast regressions.

instructions: Refactor and improve the kit to match specs and improve reusability across use cases. Componentize more of the mock-specific structures, converge visuals toward the local mocks, improve dark mode, fix accessibility issues, deepen Storybook controls and example parity, and iterate until completion.
Review the live shared files, not only the summary.
Score broadly from 0-100. Approval requires the target quality.
Focus on: componentization depth, configurable reuse, visual/spec alignment, Storybook controls, example parity, dark-mode support, and obvious a11y issues in interactive and content surfaces.
prompt.task: Review the current implementation for spec-fit, componentization quality, Storybook/example parity, dark-mode quality, and accessibility-sensitive state quality.
